|Website |[www .gene .com /scientists /our-scientists /aviv-regev](https://www.gene.com/scientists/our-scientists/aviv-regev) [](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q4829027 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

**Aviv Regev** ( Hebrew : אביב רגב; born 11 July 1971) <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> is a computational biologist and systems biologist and Executive Vice President and Head of Genentech Research and Early Development in Genentech/Roche. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> She is a core member (on leave) at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard and professor (on leave) at the Department of Biology of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> Regev is a pioneer of single cell genomics and of computational and systems biology of gene regulatory circuits . She founded and leads the Human Cell Atlas project, <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> together with Sarah Teichmann . Since 2024, she has served as a scientific advisory board member of Arc Institute . <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup>

## Education

 [edit ]

Regev studied at the Adi Lautman Interdisciplinary Program for Outstanding Students of Tel Aviv University , where she completed her PhD under the supervision of Eva Jablonka , <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> and Ehud Shapiro . <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup>

## Career and research

 [edit ]

In 2020, Regev became the head and executive vice president of Genentech Research and Early Development, based in South San Francisco, and a member of the extended corporate executive committee of Roche. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> Previously, she was a Core Institute Member (now on leave), chair of the Faculty, founding director of the Klarman Cell Observatory and co-director Cell Circuits Program at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard. She was also a professor in the Department of Biology at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (now on leave), as well as an investigator at the Howard Hughes Medical Institute. Regev's research <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup> includes work on gene expression <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup> (with Eran Segal and David Botstein ), and the use of π-calculus to represent biochemical processes. <sup>[[ 15 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 16 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 17 ]]()</sup> Regev's team has been a leading pioneer of single-cell genomics experimental and computational methods. <sup>[[ 18 ]]()</sup> In 2014, she pitched the idea of the creation of Human Cell Atlas, <sup>[[ 19 ]]()</sup> a project to describe all cell") types in the human body . Regev founded the Human Cell Atlas together with Sarah Teichmann along with collaborators all over the world. <sup>[[ 20 ]]()</sup>

### Single Cell Genomics

 [edit ]

Regev's lab pioneered the development and application of many of the key experimental and computational advances for single cell and spatial genomics, especially single cell RNA-Seq (scRNA-seq).

### Awards and honors

 [edit ]

Regev is a fellow of the International Society of Computational Biology (ISCB) (2017), <sup>[[ 21 ]]()</sup> a Helmholtz Fellow (2020), <sup>[[ 22 ]]()</sup> and a fellow of the American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) (2021). <sup>[[ 23 ]]()</sup> She is a member of the US National Academy of Sciences (NAS, elected 2019) <sup>[[ 24 ]]()</sup> and of the US National Academy of Medicine (NAM, elected 2020). <sup>[[ 25 ]]()</sup> She was elected as a foreign member of the Royal Society <sup>[[ 26 ]]()</sup> and as an associate member of the European Molecular Biology Organization in 2024. <sup>[[ 27 ]]()</sup>

* keynote address at ISSCR 2026 Annual Meeting in Montréal, 2026 <sup>[[ 28 ]]()</sup>
* National Academy of Medicine Annual Meeting Keynote, 2025 <sup>[[ 29 ]]()</sup>
* Dickson Prize in Science, 2025 <sup>[[ 30 ]]()</sup>
* William B. Coley Award for Distinguished Research in Basic and Tumor Immunology, 2025 <sup>[[ 31 ]]()</sup>
* Streisinger Memorial Lecture, 2025 <sup>[[ 32 ]]()</sup>
* Sune Bergström Award, 2025 <sup>[[ 33 ]]()</sup>
* Shai Shacknai Memorial Prize and Lectureship in Immunology and Cancer Research, 2024 <sup>[[ 34 ]]()</sup>
* TED AI 2023 Talk: Can AI Help Develop New Medicines <sup>[[ 35 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 36 ]]()</sup>
* 25th L'Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Awards (Laureate for North America), 2023 <sup>[[ 37 ]]()</sup>
* Elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, 2022 <sup>[[ 38 ]]()</sup>
* Anderson Lecture, University of Virginia, 2022 <sup>[[ 39 ]]()</sup>
* Nakasone Award from the Human Frontiers Science Program (2022) <sup>[[ 40 ]]()</sup>
* Honorary doctorate, ETH Zurich (2021) <sup>[[ 41 ]]()</sup>
* Ernst Schering Prize (2021) <sup>[[ 42 ]]()</sup>
* James Prize in Science and Technology Integration, National Academy of Sciences (2021) <sup>[[ 43 ]]()</sup>
* Vanderbilt Prize (2021) <sup>[[ 44 ]]()</sup>
* She was awarded the 25th Keio Medical Science Prize in 2020. <sup>[[ 45 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 46 ]]()</sup>
* AACR-Irving Weinstein Foundation Distinguished Lecture (2021) <sup>[[ 47 ]]()</sup>
* Lurie Prize from the Foundation for the NIH (FNIH) (2020) <sup>[[ 48 ]]()</sup>
* Mendel Lecture, European Society of Human Genetics (2020) <sup>[[ 49 ]]()</sup>
* Jonathan Kraft Prize from Massachusetts General Hospital (2020) <sup>[[ 50 ]]()</sup>
* FASEB Excellence in Science Mid-Career Investigator Award (2019) <sup>[[ 51 ]]()</sup>
* She was elected a member of the National Academy of Sciences (NAS) in 2019. <sup>[[ 52 ]]()</sup>
* She also served on the Life Sciences jury for the Infosys Prize in 2018.
* Weatherall Lecture, University Oxford, UK (2018) <sup>[[ 53 ]]()</sup>
* Harvey Lecture, Harvey Society, New York (2018) <sup>[[ 54 ]]()</sup>
* McCormick Lecture, Stanford University (2018) <sup>[[ 55 ]]()</sup>
* Paul Marks Prize for Cancer Research (2017). <sup>[[ 56 ]]()</sup>
* ISCB Innovator Award in 2017. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 57 ]]()</sup>
* Earl and Thressa Stadtman Scholar Award from the American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology (ASBMB) (2014) <sup>[[ 58 ]]()</sup>
* Burroughs Wellcome Fund Career Award. <sup>[[ 59 ]]()</sup>
* In 2008, she was also awarded the NIH Director's Pioneer Award . <sup>[[ 60 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 61 ]]()</sup>
* Regev was awarded the Overton Prize in 2008 for "outstanding accomplishment to a scientist in the early to mid stage of his or her career". <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup>
